claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do - I have a child, Alex, who's 13 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's good to know you have Alex, who is 13 and lives with you.

To help determine your eligibility, I need to ask: do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Living abroad can affect eligibility, but there are some circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in, and are you or have you been a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment. And no, I'm not a Crown servant - I'm not in the armed forces or working as a diplomat or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living in Spain and you're not a Crown servant, I need to ask a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you an EEA/Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have Alex, who is 13 years old and lives with you
- You are currently living in Spain
- You are not a Crown servant
- You do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You are not an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK

Unfortunately, this means you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The main reason is that you normally need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. While there are exceptions for people living abroad - such as Crown servants (diplomats, armed forces personnel) or those with certain immigration statuses under the EU Settlement Scheme - you do not meet these exceptions.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time, as Alex is under 16 and lives with you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements (children under 16 who live with you). However, the claimant is currently living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions for claiming Child Benefit while living abroad. According to the rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. The claimant is not a Crown servant (not in the armed forces or a diplomat), and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, nor are they an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK. Therefore, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit while living abroad in Spain.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them. While the child meets the basic age requirement (under 16), the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the qualifying conditions for claiming Child Benefit from abroad. They are not a Crown servant and do not have the necessary immigration status or right to reside that would allow them to claim while living outside the UK. Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old and lives with the claimant, which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements (children under 16 who live with you). However, the claimant is currently living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions for claiming Child Benefit while living abroad. According to the rules, you may be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant. The claimant is not a Crown servant (not in the armed forces or a diplomat), and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, nor are they an EEA or Swiss national with a right to reside in the UK. Therefore, the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit while living abroad in Spain.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them. While the child meets the basic age requirement (under 16), the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the qualifying conditions for claiming Child Benefit from abroad. They are not a Crown servant and do not have the necessary immigration status or right to reside that would allow them to claim while living outside the UK. Therefore, they are not eligible for Child Benefit.
